email-subject: Corrected! Longitudinal Study Consent Form Errors
alert-message:
Hello!

 

You are receiving this email because you consented to be contacted by the Powers lab regarding research participation, and you were recently invited to a longitudinal study.

 

Folks have brought to our attention that there were errors in the forms preventing folks from moving forward and not displaying the consent form!

 

We are so sorry for the error!

 

The form is corrected, and you can now continue at the following link:

[survey-link:consent_rpt:Consent Link (again)]

 

We have also re-attached the instructions for the study here, since folks have said that the attachment was blank or would not open before. 

 

Thank you again so much for your participation and patience! Please, please do not ever hesitate to reach out to myself or the lab if you run into issues with the study. 

------------------------------------

Max Greenwald

MD-PhD Candidate | Powers Lab

Yale School of Medicine

Phone: ‪(203) 441-7213

(he/him/his)

email-subject: Please Confirm Payment Receipt for Powers Lab Online Research Study!
alert-message:
Hello! 

 

You are receiving this email because you consented to be contacted regarding your participation in an online research study by the Powers Lab. 

 

This email is being sent to ask that you confirm that you have received your $60 compensation for your participation in the Acute Effects section of the study! If you did not receive the payment -- let us know that we need to fix this!

 

Please let us know at the following link:

[survey-link:payment_confirmation_hyp:Click here to let us know whether you received your payment]

 

Thank you again for your participation, and have a fantastic day!

 

P.S. if you used a digital US bank gift card, instructions are again attached for your convenience -- MAKE SURE YOU CLAIM YOUR CARD WITHIN 30 DAYS!!!

email-subject: Reminder: 24 Hour Time-point Computer Games for Powers Lab Online Study
alert-message:
Hello!

 

Just sending along a reminder that it is now time to complete the 24hr time-point's computer games!😊 

 

Recall, these games are designed to take 2 hours and you have until the end of the day to complete the games.

 

All you'll need is THE SAME laptop or computer (not a tablet), good internet,  THE SAME headphones, a distraction-free space, and [survey-link:acute_presurvey:this link to start the questions/games]

 

As always, if you have any questions or problems, please just email  maximillian.greenwald@yale.edu! 

 

Thank you SO much for your participation in research! 

---------------------

Max Greenwald

MD-PhD Student | Powers Lab

Yale School of Medicine
